questions.q: A 4 μF capacitor is charged by a 200 V battery. Calculate the energy stored in the capacitor.
A parallel plate capacitor has a capacitance of 100 pF and a potential difference of 100 V across it. What is the charge stored on the capacitor?
A conducting sphere carries a charge of 1.6 × 10^-7 C. What is its electrostatic potential?
Three capacitors, each of capacitance 100 pF, are connected in series. What is the equivalent capacitance of the combination?
A capacitor of capacitance 2 μF is connected across a battery of 12 V. What is the charge on the capacitor?
A 10 μF capacitor is charged to a potential difference of 50 V. Calculate the energy stored in the capacitor.
Two capacitors of capacitance 100 pF each are connected in parallel. What is the equivalent capacitance of the combination?
A parallel plate capacitor has a capacitance of 2 nF and is connected across a battery of 150 V. Calculate the energy stored in the capacitor.
A spherical capacitor has a radius of 10 cm and a potential difference of 100 V across it. What is the capacitance of the capacitor?
A 20 μF capacitor is connected across a 50 V battery. Calculate the charge on the capacitor and the energy stored in it.
